projectContext.name: AuthorWebsites - Multi-Tenant Static Site Generator
projectContext.description: Eleventy-based static site generator for fiction authors with multiple pen names. Supports unlimited author identities from a single codebase with Decap CMS for content management.
criticalFiles: src/_data/profiles.js - Pen name profiles (active name set via ELEVENTY_PEN_NAME env var)
scripts/build-all-sites.js - Multi-site build automation (sets ELEVENTY_PEN_NAME per build)
keyArchitectureNotes: ELEVENTY_PEN_NAME env var controls which site builds (defaults to first profile)
Each pen name requires: outputFolder, styles (CSS), blogFolder, and optional bookList (JSON)
build-all-sites.js sets ELEVENTY_PEN_NAME for each build iteration
Each pen name builds to a separate _site_* folder (gitignored)
Blog posts stored in src/blogs/penname/ directories
Book data stored in src/_data/penname_books.json files

commonCommands.start: Start dev server with live reload
commonCommands.build:all: Build all pen name sites to separate folders

agentGuidelines: Always validate profiles.js before making changes to pen names. Active pen name is set via ELEVENTY_PEN_NAME env var.
When adding a new pen name, ensure CSS file, blog folder, and books JSON are created
Never commit _site_* folders (they are build artifacts)
Test builds locally with 'npm run build:all' before deploying
The build-all-sites.js script sets ELEVENTY_PEN_NAME for each pen name during builds
Blog posts use frontmatter with: title, date, author, excerpt, tags
Books data uses JSON with: title, isbn, coverImage, description, purchaseLinks
Deployment configs (netlify-config.json, deploy-config.json) are gitignored - only commit examples

preDeployment: Run 'npm run build:all' and verify no errors
Check that all _site_* folders are generated
Ensure images are optimized and load correctly
